meta.name: Seasonal Campaigns
meta.description: Tradition-tagged seasonal campaigns. Each season references a quest-series and a continuity ring. Dates are descriptive (lunar/computed feasts vary by year); the engine reads `window` hints, never assumes one tradition's calendar as default.

seasons.title: Advent
seasons.subtitle: Four weeks of waiting toward the Nativity
seasons.intel: Advent traces the long expectation. The Messiah thread is the spine; Wisdom personified is the antiphon.

seasons.title: Lent
seasons.subtitle: Forty days toward the Passion
seasons.intel: Lent is forty days of descent. The Fathers on the Incarnation and the 'I AM' sayings prepare the reader for the cross.

seasons.title: Eastertide
seasons.subtitle: Fifty days of resurrection light
seasons.intel: Fifty days from Resurrection to Pentecost. The light sayings and the catch of 153 fish carry the post-Easter narrative.

seasons.title: Elul → High Holidays
seasons.subtitle: A month of return toward the Days of Awe
seasons.intel: Elul through Yom Kippur is the season of teshuvah (return). The Names of God and the opening of Berakhot anchor the daily turning.

seasons.title: Daf Yomi
seasons.subtitle: One folio of Talmud a day, the full Shas in cycle
seasons.intel: The seven-and-a-half-year cycle through the Babylonian Talmud, one daf per day. Continuity here is the discipline of the page.

seasons.title: Tehillim — Monthly
seasons.subtitle: The whole Psalter across a Hebrew month
seasons.intel: The traditional division of Tehillim across thirty days. A continuity ring sized to the lunar month.

seasons.title: CODEX Annual — Ninety Days Through the Whole Canon
seasons.subtitle: The entire canon, apocrypha included, in ninety days
seasons.intel: The flagship campaign: ninety days across the whole canon including the apocrypha. Six quests sequence the spine; the continuity ring spans the full ninety.

seasons.title: The Hidden Reading
seasons.subtitle: A self-paced arc through the gospels of light
seasons.intel: A tradition-honest gnostic arc. Self-paced, thirty-three days, framed as one lens among many.
